what_this_pass_proved: Star ratings at session end give the player a visible target beyond just clearing incidents — the run now answers 'How did I do?' with a grade.
Incident personality lines (unique dispatch and resolved text per incident type) make each call feel like a distinct event rather than a generic math prompt.
The debrief shows resolved incidents, timeouts, overshoot attempts, response time, hardest demands, and one practical takeaway — the player leaves knowing what to improve.
open_questions: Are star thresholds tuned correctly for the dispatch context? Too easy and the rating is meaningless; too hard and it discourages replay.
Does the takeaway feel specific enough to change behavior on the next run?

bugs_fixed.description: No end-of-session feedback existed beyond a score total. Added star rating, incident log summary, and contextual takeaway.

known_limits: Star rating is per-session aggregate. Does not break down performance by incident type or level.
Debrief persistence is session-only. No cross-session comparison.
next_pass_first_obligation: Verify that the star rating and debrief produce a reason to replay immediately — does the player know what to do differently on the next run?
